skins.title: Login Skins
skins.updated_success: The skin has been updated. (The cache was also cleared automatically.)
skins.placeholder_form_action: Form action (POSTs to the current URL if empty)
skins.placeholder_skin_hint: Skin hint value (used in a hidden input)
skins.guide_subtitle: Fetch HTML from an external URL to fully customize the login and sign-up screens.
skins.guide_overview_desc: The IDP lets you register custom skin URLs for the following four pages per OIDC client / SAML SP. When a skin URL is registered, the IDP fetches the URL and returns its HTML when the page is accessed. If none is registered or the fetch fails, the built-in default UI is shown.
skins.guide_flow_1: When a user starts an OIDC/SAML login flow, the IDP redirects to the login page.
skins.guide_flow_2: The IDP looks up the requested client's skin settings in the database.
skins.guide_flow_3: If a skin URL exists, it checks the R2 cache; if the TTL has expired or there is no cache, it fetches the HTML from the skin server.
skins.guide_flow_4: The IDP replaces placeholders in the HTML (such as {{IDP_FORM_ACTION}}) with actual values and returns it to the browser.
skins.guide_flow_5: When the user submits the skin form, the IDP processes it and redirects to the client.
skins.guide_auth_desc: If you set an authentication secret on the skin URL, the IDP includes that value in the X-IDP-Token header when fetching. The skin server can verify this header to confirm the request came from the IDP.
skins.guide_example_login_desc: Below is a minimal implementation example of a login skin. It works as long as it includes {{IDP_FORM_ACTION}} and the two hidden inputs.
skins.guide_example_note_1: The form method must be POST.
skins.guide_example_note_2: You must use the exact field names username and password.
skins.guide_example_note_3: Without the redirectTo and skinHint hidden inputs, the post-login redirect may not work.
skins.guide_cache_1: Skin HTML is cached in Cloudflare R2 and reused for the configured TTL (seconds).
skins.guide_cache_2: When the TTL expires, the next request fetches fresh HTML from the skin server and refreshes the cache.
skins.guide_cache_3: Click the 'Clear Cache' button on the skin list page to invalidate the cache immediately.
skins.guide_setup_1: Prepare a server (or CDN) to host the skin HTML and obtain a public URL.
skins.guide_setup_2: If authentication is required, decide on a secret value and add logic to your server to verify the X-IDP-Token header.
skins.guide_setup_3: On the Login Skins management page, enter the target client, skin type, URL, secret, and TTL, and add it.
skins.guide_setup_4: After adding, enable it with the toggle to apply the custom skin in that client's login flow.
skins.guide_code_comment: // Example request verification on the skin server (Node.js)
